attempts.description: flash_decode_attention_whole_cache with DECODE_ATTN_BLOCK_TILE=1; target_s=48 default gives l_route=96, s_route=6 at ctx=512
flash_decode_g5_block_tile direct dispatch with l_route=L=128, s_route=ceildiv(ctx,L)=4, grid=8x4=32 workgroups at ctx=512

root_cause: Per-workgroup kernel cost is the bottleneck, not occupancy. Block tile kernel (flash_block_tiled_xlane_score_pv_tile_whole_cache_40_128) takes ~2090us vs flash_partial_coop_vec_40_128 at 27us — 77x slower per workgroup. Reading both K and V into LDS doubles BW vs baseline, and cross-warp accumulation for G=5 GQA groups adds overhead. Sliced routing (32 WGs) is marginally slower than whole-cache (48 WGs) because the per-WG cost dominates. 40 layers x 2090us = 83.6ms overhead vs baseline step ~20ms.
reopen_condition: Reopen only if: (a) native RDNA3 ISA block tile implementation achieves per-kernel time <100us (vs 2090us current), or (b) profiling reveals a specific tinygrad codegen pathology (scalar loads, register spilling, excessive barrier synchronization) with a targeted fix that reaches <50us per kernel call.
what_stays: WARPS=G parameterization in flash_block_tiled_xlane_score_pv_tile_whole_cache_kernel — G=4 unchanged, no regression on 8B
DECODE_FLASH_BLOCK_TILE_G5=0 gate + flash_decode_g5_block_tile function — default-off, no performance impact unless set
